title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but could be exploited by malicious actors to identify and target unpatched vulnerabilities in systems using the tool. The tool's widespread adoption could also lead to over-reliance without proper validation of its results.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and functionality before integrating it into workflows to ensure it aligns with organizational security policies.
Monitor for any potential misuse of the tool by malicious actors and ensure all dependencies are patched promptly.
Educate teams on the importance of verifying the results of automated tools and not solely relying on them for vulnerability management.

title: The LatePoint plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Account Takeover via Weak Password Recovery...
description: The LatePoint plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Account Takeover via Weak Password Recovery Mechanism in the unauthenticated guest booking flow in versions up to, and including, 5.5.0 This is due to the save_connected_wordpress_user() function propagating a LatePoint customer's email address to its linked WordPress user account via wp_update_user() without any ownership verification, combined with the guest booking flow's ability to overwrite an existing customer's email through phone-based

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7652.

title: Gibbon versions before v30.0.01 are affected by an authenticated SQL Injection vulnerability by...
description: Gibbon versions before v30.0.01 are affected by an authenticated SQL Injection vulnerability by abusing the  Tracking/graphing https://github.com/GibbonEdu/core/blob/c431e25fdc874adece5d2dc7e408e9aa2d1abadb/modules/Tracking/graphing.php#L145  feature. Successful exploitation requires Teacher or higher privileges. Exploitation could result in unintended read/write activities to the underlying database.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users with Teacher or higher privileges to perform SQL Injection attacks, potentially leading to unauthorized read/write access to the database. This could result in data breaches, data manipulation, or system compromise.
affected_industries: Organizations using Gibbon for school management
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Gibbon version v30.0.01 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict access to accounts with Teacher or higher privileges and enforce strong password policies.
Monitor database activity for any suspicious or unauthorized queries and enable logging for auditing purposes.

title: PgBouncer before 1.25.2 did not perform an appropriate authorization check for the KILL_CLIENT...
description: PgBouncer before 1.25.2 did not perform an appropriate authorization check for the KILL_CLIENT admin command. All users with access to the administration console (which itself requires authorization) could run this command. It would have been correct to allow only users listed in the admin_users parameter.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6667.

title: A possible null pointer reference in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 could lead to a crash, if a server...
description: A possible null pointer reference in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 could lead to a crash, if a server sends an error response without SQLSTATE field.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6666.

title: The SCRAM code in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 did not check the return value of strlcat() correctly...
description: The SCRAM code in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 did not check the return value of strlcat() correctly when building the contents of the SCRAM client-final-message. A malicious backend that sends a SCRAM server-final-message with a long nonce can trigger a stack overflow.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in PgBouncer's SCRAM authentication mechanism can be exploited by a malicious backend to trigger a stack overflow, potentially leading to denial of service (DoS) or remote code execution (RCE). This poses a significant risk to systems relying on PgBouncer for database connection pooling.
recommended_actions: Immediately update PgBouncer to version 1.25.2 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Review and monitor backend connections for any suspicious activity or malformed SCRAM messages.
Implement additional network security measures, such as firewalls or intrusion detection systems, to mitigate potential exploitation attempts.

title: An integer overflow in network packet parsing code in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 bypasses a boundary...
description: An integer overflow in network packet parsing code in PgBouncer before 1.25.2 bypasses a boundary check and can lead to a crash. An unauthenticated remote attacker can crash PgBouncer with a malformed SCRAM authentication packet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to crash PgBouncer by exploiting an integer overflow in the network packet parsing code. This can lead to denial of service (DoS), disrupting database connection pooling and potentially impacting dependent applications.
recommended_actions: Upgrade PgBouncer to version 1.25.2 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement network-level protections, such as firewalls or intrusion detection systems, to monitor and block malformed SCRAM authentication packets.
Conduct a review of access controls and ensure only trusted sources can communicate with PgBouncer.

title: Spring AI's MilvusVectorStore#doDelete(List) implementation is vulnerable to filter-expression...
description:
Spring AI's MilvusVectorStore#doDelete(List) implementation is vulnerable to filter-expression injection via unsanitized document IDs.
Spring AI 1.0.x: affected from 1.0.0 through latest 1.0.x; upgrade to 1.0.7 or greater. Spring AI 1.1.x: affected from 1.1.0 through latest 1.1.x; upgrade to 1.1.6 or greater.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41705.

title: Hono has CSS Declaration Injection via Style Object Values in JSX SSR
description:
### Summary

The JSX renderer escapes `style` attribute object values for HTML but not for CSS. Untrusted input in a `style` object value or property name can therefore inject additional CSS declarations into the rendered `style` attribute. The impact is limited to CSS and does not allow JavaScript execution or HTML attribute breakout.

### Details

`style` object values are serialized into a CSS declaration list and escaped for HTML attribute context only. Characters that act as CSS declaration

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44458.

title: Hono has improper validation of NumericDate claims (exp, nbf, iat) in JWT verify()
description:
### Summary

Improper validation of the JWT NumericDate claims `exp`, `nbf`, and `iat` in `hono/utils/jwt` allows tokens with non-spec-compliant claim values to silently bypass time-based checks. This issue is not exploitable by an anonymous attacker; it only manifests when a malformed claim value reaches `verify()` — typically when the application itself issues such tokens, or when the signing key is otherwise under attacker control.

### Details

The validation routine combined option, presenc

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows improperly formatted JWT tokens with non-compliant NumericDate claims (exp, nbf, iat) to bypass time-based validation checks. This could lead to unauthorized access or misuse of expired or invalid tokens if the application issues malformed tokens or if the signing key is compromised.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of the Hono library where this issue is patched.
Implement strict validation of JWT claims before issuing tokens to ensure compliance with the specification.
Regularly rotate and securely store signing keys to minimize the risk of unauthorized key access.

title: @profullstack/mcp-server vulnerable to OS Command Injection in domain_lookup Module
description:
Security Advisory: OS Command Injection in profullstack/mcp-server domain_lookup Module

Field | Value -- | -- Project | profullstack/mcp-server Repository | https://github.com/profullstack/mcp-server Affected Commit | 2e8ea913573610667ad54e31dba2e8198ebf7cf9 Affected Module | mcp_modules/domain_lookup Affected Endpoints | POST /domain-lookup/check, POST /domain-lookup/bulk Vulnerability Type | CWE-78:

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands via the domain_lookup module, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the profullstack/mcp-server package to the latest patched version if available.
Implement input validation and sanitization on all endpoints to prevent command injection.
Conduct a security audit of the affected endpoints and monitor for signs of exploitation.

title: Velocity.js has a Prototype Pollution vulnerability through #set path assignment
description:
### Summary
A prototype pollution vulnerability was discovered in Velocity.js <= 2.1.5. This issue occurs during the processing of #set directives in Velocity templates. If an application renders a template controlled by an attacker, it is possible to modify Object.prototype, potentially leading to Denial of Service (DoS) or Remote Code Execution (RCE) depending on the server environment.

### Details
The root cause is located in the #set path assignment logic within the source code:
- File: /sr

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44966.

title: Vert.x has a DoS via unbounded server-side SNI SslContext cache growth
description:
Potential unbounded server-side SNI `SslContext` cache growth in Vert.x TLS handling, with possible resource-exhaustion / DoS impact.

On affected versions, matching server-side SNI names are cached via `computeIfAbsent(serverName, ...)` in a serverName-keyed `SslContext` cache, and I could not find any bound, TTL, or eviction for that cache.

The implementation differs slightly by branch, but the same sink appears to be present in released versions `4.3.4` through `5.0.8`:
- `4.3.x`: `SSLHelper

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6860.

title: Hono's Cache Middleware ignores Vary: Authorization / Vary: Cookie leading to cross-user cache leakage
description:
### Summary

Cache Middleware does not skip caching for responses that declare per-user variance via `Vary: Authorization` or `Vary: Cookie`. As a result, a response cached for one authenticated user may be served to subsequent requests from different users.

### Details

The Cache Middleware skips caching when a response carries `Vary: *`, certain `Cache-Control` directives (`private`, `no-store`, `no-cache`), or `Set-Cookie`. However, `Vary: Authorization` and `Vary: Cookie` — the standard sig

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44457.

title: Mistune Heading ID Attribute has Injection XSS
description:
## Summary
`HTMLRenderer.heading()` builds the opening `<hN>` tag by string-concatenating the `id` attribute value directly into the HTML — with no call to `escape()`, `safe_entity()`, or any other sanitisation function. A double-quote character `"` in the `id` value terminates the attribute, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary additional attributes (event handlers, `src=`, `href=`, etc.) into the heading element.

The default TOC hook assigns safe auto-incremented IDs (`toc_1`, `toc_2`, …)

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44897.

title: @yoda.digital/gitlab-mcp-server's SSE transport has no authentication and wildcard CORS, exposing all 86 GitLab tools
description:
## SSE Transport Has No Authentication and Wildcard CORS, Exposing All 86 GitLab Tools Including Destructive Operations

A review of `mcp-gitlab-server` at commit `80a7b4cf3fba6b55389c0ef491a48190f7c8996a` uncovered that the SSE HTTP transport — advertised in the README and comparison table as a differentiating feature — runs with no authentication and wildcard CORS on every endpoint. The maintainers' own roadmap confirms auth is a known gap.

When `USE_SSE=true`, the HTTP server in `src/transpo

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The lack of authentication and wildcard CORS in the SSE transport exposes all 86 GitLab tools, including destructive operations, to unauthorized access. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized code execution, and compromise of sensitive repositories.
recommended_actions: Immediately disable the SSE transport feature (`USE_SSE=true`) in the affected GitLab server until proper authentication mechanisms are implemented.
Implement strict authentication and authorization checks for all endpoints, ensuring only authorized users can access the SSE transport.
Restrict CORS settings to only allow trusted origins and remove wildcard configurations to prevent unauthorized cross-origin requests.

title: smallbitvec: Integer overflow in safe API leads to heap buffer overflow
description:
### Summary
An integer overflow in the internal capacity calculation of `smallbitvec` can lead to an undersized heap allocation, resulting in a heap buffer overflow through safe APIs only. This allows memory corruption without requiring `unsafe` code from the caller.

### Details
The issue originates from unchecked arithmetic in the internal helper function responsible for computing the required buffer size:

```
(cap + bits_per_storage() - 1) / bits_per_storage()
```

When `cap` is close to `us

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44983.

title: SharpCompress has directory traversal via directory entries in WriteToDirectory (zip slip variant)
description:
### Summary

A path traversal vulnerability in `IArchive.WriteToDirectory()` allows a malicious archive to create directories outside the intended extraction root. For TAR archives, this can be escalated to arbitrary file writes by chaining with a symlink entry, giving a full write primitive on the target filesystem subject to the permissions of the running process.

### Details

The vulnerable code is in the directory-entry branch of `WriteToDirectoryInternal` (sync, `IArchiveExtensions.cs:48–6

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit path traversal in SharpCompress's WriteToDirectory method, potentially leading to unauthorized file writes outside the intended directory. For TAR archives, this can be escalated to arbitrary file writes, enabling attackers to overwrite critical system files or inject malicious files, depending on the permissions of the running process.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest version of SharpCompress that addresses CVE-2026-44788, or apply any available patches from the vendor.
Implement additional input validation and sanitization to ensure extracted file paths are confined to the intended directory.
Run applications using SharpCompress with the least privileges necessary to minimize the impact of potential exploitation.

title: epa4all-client has a VAU Signature bypass
description:
### Impact
In SignedPublicKeysTrustValidatorImpl.isTrusted(), the ECDSA signature verification at line 45 discards the boolean return value of Signature.verify(). The method performs certificate chain validation, OCSP check, and signature algorithm setup, but never checks whether the signature actually matches. For any structurally valid signature, it returns true.

### Patches
Patched in [#34](https://github.com/oviva-ag/epa4all-client/pull/34).

### Workarounds
None.

### Resources
- [MS-OVIVA

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44900.

title: Mistune has XSS via unescaped figclass/figwidth in Figure directive
description:
In `src/mistune/directives/image.py`, the `render_figure()` function concatenates `figclass` and `figwidth` options directly into HTML attributes without escaping (lines 152-168).

This allows attribute injection and XSS even when `HTMLRenderer(escape=True)` is used, because these values bypass the inline renderer.

Other attributes in the same file (src, alt, style) are properly escaped; figclass/figwidth were missed.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44896.

title: Mistune Math Plugin has an XSS Escape Bypass
description:
## Summary
The mistune math plugin renders inline math (`$...$`) and block math (`$$...$$`) by concatenating the raw user-supplied content directly into the HTML output **without any HTML escaping**. This occurs even when the parser is explicitly created with `escape=True`, which is supposed to guarantee that all user-controlled text is sanitised before reaching the DOM.

The result is a silent contract violation: a developer who enables `escape=True` reasonably expects complete XSS protection,

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44708.

title: CVE-2026-7604 - A vulnerability was identified in JeecgBoot up to 3.9.1. This affects the function OpenApiController
description: A vulnerability was identified in JeecgBoot up to 3.9.1. This affects the function OpenApiController.add/OpenApiController.call of the file OpenApiController.java of the component OpenApi Service. Such manipulation of the argument originUrl database leads to server-side request forgery. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. It is suggested to upgrade the affected component. The vendor confirmed the issue and will provide a fix in the upcoming release.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in JeecgBoot's OpenApiController allows for server-side request forgery (SSRF), which could enable attackers to manipulate requests to internal or external systems, potentially leading to data exfiltration, unauthorized access, or further exploitation of internal systems. The exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Upgrade JeecgBoot to the latest version as soon as the vendor releases a patch addressing this vulnerability.
Implement input validation and sanitization for the 'originUrl' parameter to mitigate exploitation of the SSRF vulnerability.
Monitor network traffic for unusual or unauthorized outbound requests to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-7603 - A vulnerability was determined in JeecgBoot up to 3.9.1. Affected by this issue is the function chec
description: A vulnerability was determined in JeecgBoot up to 3.9.1. Affected by this issue is the function checkPathTraversalBatch of the file FileDownloadUtils.jav of the component LoadFile Endpoint. This manipulation of the argument files causes server-side request forgery. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The affected component should be upgraded. The vendor confirmed the issue and will provide a fix in the upcoming release.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7603.

title: CVE-2026-7458 - The User Verification by PickPlugins plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authentication bypass in
description: The User Verification by PickPlugins plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authentication bypass in all versions up to, and including, 2.0.46. This is due to the use of a loose PHP comparison operator to validate OTP codes in the "user_verification_form_wrap_process_otpLogin" function. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to log in as any user with a verified email address, such as an administrator, by submitting a "true" OTP value.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication and gain unauthorized access to WordPress accounts, including administrative accounts. This can lead to full website compromise, data breaches, defacement, and potential lateral movement within the affected organization's network.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the User Verification by PickPlugins plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable the plugin until a fix is released.
Implement additional security measures, such as Web Application Firewalls (WAF) and two-factor authentication (2FA), to mitigate the risk of unauthorized access.
Review and monitor user accounts for signs of unauthorized access, especially administrative accounts, and reset passwords if suspicious activity is detected.

title: CVE-2026-6963 - The WP Mail Gateway plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access due to a missing capab
description: The WP Mail Gateway plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access due to a missing capability check on the wmg_save_provider_config AJAX action in all versions up to, and including, 1.8. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to update SMTP settings and redirect mail which can be used for privilege escalation by triggering a password reset email and using that to access and administrator's account.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6963.

title: CVE-2026-6446 - The My Social Feeds – Social Feeds Embedder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Informat
description: The My Social Feeds – Social Feeds Embedder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in all versions up to and including 1.0.4 via the 'ttp_get_accounts' AJAX action. This is due to the complete absence of authorization checks (no capability verification) and nonce verification in the get_accounts() function, which returns the full contents of the 'ttp_tiktok_accounts' WordPress option. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to retrieve sensitive TikTok OAuth credentials, including access_token and refresh_token values, that belong to administrator-connected TikTok accounts, enabling them to impersonate the site owner when interacting with the TikTok API.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6446.

title: CVE-2026-4882 - The User Registration Advanced Fields plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file uploads d
description: The User Registration Advanced Fields plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file uploads due to missing file type validation in the 'URAF_AJAX::method_upload' function in all versions up to, and including, 1.6.20. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to upload arbitrary files on the affected site's server which may make remote code execution possible. Note: The vulnerability can only be exploited if a "Profile Picture" field is added to the form.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4882.

title: CVE-2026-4658 - The Essential Blocks – Page Builder Gutenberg Blocks, Patterns & Templates plugin for WordPress is v
description: The Essential Blocks – Page Builder Gutenberg Blocks, Patterns & Templates plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the className, classHook, and blockId attributes in the Add to Cart block (essential-blocks/add-to-cart) in all versions up to, and including, 6.0.4. This is due to insufficient output escaping in the render_callback() function where these attributes are placed into class and data-id HTML attributes using raw sprintf() and implode() without esc_attr() escaping. While the outer wrapper div uses get_block_wrapper_attributes() which properly escapes, the inner divs do not. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4658.

title: CVE-2025-14726 - The Widgets for Social Photo Feed plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access of data
description: The Widgets for Social Photo Feed plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access of data and modification of data due to a missing capability check on the '/trustindex_feed_hook_instagram/troubleshooting' and '/trustindex_feed_hook_instagram/submit-data' REST API endpoints in all versions up to, and including, 1.8. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to access and update plugin settings.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-14726.

title: CVE-2026-7638 - The App Builder – Create Native Android & iOS Apps On The Flight plugin for WordPress is vulnerable
description: The App Builder – Create Native Android & iOS Apps On The Flight plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference  in all versions up to and including 5.6.0. This is due to missing authorization validation in the `upload_avatar()` function, which accepts an attacker-controlled `user_id` parameter from the POST request body and uses it to update user meta without verifying that the authenticated requester owns or has permission to modify the target account. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to overwrite the profile avatar of any arbitrary user on the site, including administrators, by supplying a target `user_id` in the request body to the `/wp-json/app-builder/v1/upload-avatar` endpoint.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7638.

title: CVE-2026-7602 - A vulnerability was found in JeecgBoot up to 3.9.1. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown fun
description: A vulnerability was found in JeecgBoot up to 3.9.1. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the file /sys/fillRule/edit of the component FillRuleUtil Component. The manipulation of the argument ruleClass results in improper authorization. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made public and could be used. You should upgrade the affected component. The vendor confirmed the issue and will provide a fix in the upcoming release.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7602.

title: CVE-2026-7209 - The Simple Link Directory plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the
description: The Simple Link Directory plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin's `qcopd-directory` shortcode in all versions up to, and including, 8.9.2. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied attributes such as `title_font_size`. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7209.

title: CVE-2026-6378 - The Maxi Blocks plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the `/wp-json/
description: The Maxi Blocks plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the `/wp-json/maxi-blocks/v1.0/style-card` REST API endpoint in all versions up to, and including, 2.1.9 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping of the `sc_styles` parameter. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Author-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts that execute on every page where the plugin's style card styles are loaded, including across the entire WordPress admin panel.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6378.

title: CVE-2026-7601 - A vulnerability has been found in Open5GS up to 2.7.6. Affected is an unknown function of the file s
description: A vulnerability has been found in Open5GS up to 2.7.6. Affected is an unknown function of the file src/amf/gmm-handler.c of the component AMF. The manipulation of the argument reg_type leads to denial of service. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. Upgrading to version 2.7.7 is able to address this issue. The identifier of the patch is ebc66942b6f8f1fab2d640e71cf4e9f1a423b426. It is advisable to upgrade the affected component.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7601.

title: CVE-2026-43824 - In Argo CD 3.2.0 before 3.2.11 and 3.3.0 before 3.3.9, ServerSideDiff allows reading cleartext Kuber
description: In Argo CD 3.2.0 before 3.2.11 and 3.3.0 before 3.3.9, ServerSideDiff allows reading cleartext Kubernetes Secret data.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-43824.

title: CVE-2026-7600 - A flaw has been found in ArtMin96 yii2-mcp-server 1.0.2. This impacts the function yii_command_help/
description: A flaw has been found in ArtMin96 yii2-mcp-server 1.0.2. This impacts the function yii_command_help/yii_execute_command of the file src/index.ts of the component MCP Interface. Executing a manipulation can lead to os command injection. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7600.
